rulings.Case Title: GRADY FRAZIER VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Joinder In Boeing Companys Motion To Dismiss For Failure To Prosecute Claim
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, June 27, 2013 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Ingersoll Rand Co.¿s joinder in Boeing Co.¿s motion to dismiss for failure to prosecute pursuant to C.C.P. 583.410-583.430 is denied as untimely. Defendant failed to provide proper notice to plaintiff as required by CRC 3.1342(a) and C.C.P. 1005(b). The joinder is denied without prejudice to defendant filing its own motion and providing proper notice to Mr. Frazier. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: GRADY FRAZIER VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Amended Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss For Failure To Prosecute Claim
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, June 27, 2013 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant The Boeing Company¿s motion to dismiss for failure to prosecute pursuant to C.C.P. 583.410-583.430 is granted. No opposition filed. Hearing required unless counsel for Boeing notifies Mr. Frazier of this tentative ruling and he states that he does not wish to contest. The hearing will be at 9:45a.m. Defendant must bring a proposed order to the hearing. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: ROBERT JAMES HANSON VS. SEQUOIA VENTURES, INC.
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, June 27, 2013 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 2. Defendant Cordova Chemical Co.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Plaintiff¿s deposition testimony and responses to defendant¿s special interrogatories preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that it cannot be liable under plaintiff¿s premise owner/contractor liability cause of action. Defendant¿s alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to the premise owner/contractor liability cause of action for the same reasons summary judgment is denied and is off calendar as to all other causes of action since they were not alleged against this defendant. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
